US-3.1-01-map-loaded.png.action: The user opens the thematic map page
US-3.1-01-map-loaded.png.expected: A dark background map canvas loads successfully

US-3.1-02-clusters-visible.png.action: The user observes the map SVG area
US-3.1-02-clusters-visible.png.expected: Colored cluster regions and researcher dots are visible on the map

US-3.1-03-filter-panel.png.action: The user observes the filter panel
US-3.1-03-filter-panel.png.expected: A floating panel shows Theme and Lab dropdowns with an Apply button

US-3.1-04-legend.png.action: The user observes the map legend
US-3.1-04-legend.png.expected: A bottom-right legend lists cluster colors paired with theme names

US-3.1-05-zoom-in.png.action: The user scrolls to zoom in on the map
US-3.1-05-zoom-in.png.expected: The map zooms in and clusters and dots appear larger

US-3.1-06-pan.png.action: The user clicks and drags the map to pan
US-3.1-06-pan.png.expected: The map viewport shifts to reveal a different area

US-3.1-07-filter-applied.png.action: The user selects a theme in the filter and clicks Apply
US-3.1-07-filter-applied.png.expected: Only the selected theme's cluster and its researchers remain visible

US-3.1-08-crossnav-list.png.action: The user clicks the List View cross-navigation button
US-3.1-08-crossnav-list.png.expected: The application navigates to the themes list page

US-3.1-E1-01-intercept.png.action: The system intercepts the clusters API to return an empty list
US-3.1-E1-01-intercept.png.expected: The API is configured to respond with no cluster data

US-3.1-E1-02-map-loaded.png.action: The user navigates to the map with no cluster data available
US-3.1-E1-02-map-loaded.png.expected: The map page loads with its dark background container

US-3.1-E1-03-empty-state.png.action: The user observes the map area when no clusters exist
US-3.1-E1-03-empty-state.png.expected: An empty state message is centered on the dark map background

US-3.1-E2-01-map-loaded.png.action: The user opens the map page
US-3.1-E2-01-map-loaded.png.expected: The map loads and displays clusters normally

US-3.1-E2-02-zooming.png.action: The user scrolls to zoom in repeatedly
US-3.1-E2-02-zooming.png.expected: The map progressively zooms in with each scroll action

US-3.1-E2-03-max-zoom.png.action: The user attempts to zoom in further at maximum zoom
US-3.1-E2-03-max-zoom.png.expected: The zoom level is capped and the view no longer changes

US-3.1-E3-01-intercept.png.action: The system intercepts the clusters API to simulate a timeout error
US-3.1-E3-01-intercept.png.expected: The API is configured to respond with a gateway timeout

US-3.1-E3-02-map-loaded.png.action: The user navigates to the map while the API is failing
US-3.1-E3-02-map-loaded.png.expected: The map page starts to load

US-3.1-E3-03-error-retry.png.action: The user observes the map area after a load failure
US-3.1-E3-03-error-retry.png.expected: An error message with a retry button is shown centered on the map

US-3.2-01-map-loaded.png.action: The user opens the thematic map page
US-3.2-01-map-loaded.png.expected: The map loads and displays colored cluster regions

US-3.2-02-popover-open.png.action: The user clicks on a cluster region on the map
US-3.2-02-popover-open.png.expected: A popover appears listing the member researchers of that cluster

US-3.2-03-popover-content.png.action: The user observes the cluster popover content
US-3.2-03-popover-content.png.expected: Theme tags and researcher names displayed as clickable links

US-3.2-04-navigate-profile.png.action: The user clicks a researcher name in the cluster popover
US-3.2-04-navigate-profile.png.expected: The application navigates to that researcher's profile page

US-3.2-E1-01-intercept.png.action: The system intercepts cluster data to include a cluster with 55 members
US-3.2-E1-01-intercept.png.expected: The API is configured to return an oversized member list

US-3.2-E1-02-popover-open.png.action: The user clicks the large cluster on the map

US-3.2-E1-03-truncated.png.action: The user observes the popover for a cluster with many members
US-3.2-E1-03-truncated.png.expected: The popover shows the first members with a link indicating additional ones

US-3.2-E2-01-popover-open.png.action: The user opens a cluster popover by clicking a cluster region
US-3.2-E2-01-popover-open.png.expected: The popover is visible over the map

US-3.2-E2-02-popover-closed.png.action: The user clicks the dark map background outside the popover
US-3.2-E2-02-popover-closed.png.expected: The popover closes and the map is visible without any overlay

US-3.2-E3-01-intercept.png.action: The system intercepts the cluster members endpoint with a slow delay
US-3.2-E3-01-intercept.png.expected: The API is configured to respond slowly for member data

US-3.2-E3-02-popover-open.png.action: The user clicks a cluster while member data is loading slowly
US-3.2-E3-02-popover-open.png.expected: The cluster popover opens while awaiting member data

US-3.2-E3-03-loading.png.action: The user observes the popover while member data is still loading
US-3.2-E3-03-loading.png.expected: A loading spinner is visible inside the popover

US-3.3-01-map-loaded.png.action: The user opens the thematic map page
US-3.3-01-map-loaded.png.expected: The map loads and researcher dots are visible across the canvas

US-3.3-02-hover-tooltip.png.action: The user hovers over a researcher dot on the map
US-3.3-02-hover-tooltip.png.expected: A tooltip with the researcher's name appears and the cursor changes to a pointer

US-3.3-03-navigate-profile.png.action: The user clicks a researcher dot on the map
US-3.3-03-navigate-profile.png.expected: The application navigates to that researcher's profile page

US-3.3-E1-01-intercept.png.action: The system intercepts cluster data so two researchers share the same position
US-3.3-E1-01-intercept.png.expected: The API is configured to place two researcher dots at identical coordinates

US-3.3-E1-02-disambiguation.png.action: The user clicks on overlapping researcher dots
US-3.3-E1-02-disambiguation.png.expected: A disambiguation popover lists the names of both researchers

US-3.3-E1-03-selected.png.action: The user clicks one name in the disambiguation popover
US-3.3-E1-03-selected.png.expected: The application navigates to that specific researcher's profile

US-3.3-E2-01-intercept.png.action: The system intercepts the researcher profile API to return an error
US-3.3-E2-01-intercept.png.expected: The API is configured to fail when a researcher profile is requested

US-3.3-E2-02-click-dot.png.action: The user clicks a researcher dot on the map
US-3.3-E2-02-click-dot.png.expected: The application attempts to navigate to the researcher's profile

US-3.3-E2-03-toast.png.action: The user observes the result after clicking an unavailable researcher
US-3.3-E2-03-toast.png.expected: A toast notification appears indicating the profile is unavailable

US-3.3-E3-01-zoomed-out.png.action: The user zooms the map out to its minimum zoom level
US-3.3-E3-01-zoomed-out.png.expected: The map is displayed at minimum zoom with small researcher dots

US-3.3-E3-02-touch-target.png.action: The user inspects a researcher dot at full zoom out
US-3.3-E3-02-touch-target.png.expected: Each dot maintains a minimum clickable area regardless of visual size

US-3.4-01-themes-loaded.png.action: The user opens the themes explorer page
US-3.4-01-themes-loaded.png.expected: The page loads with the heading and a grid of theme cluster cards

US-3.4-02-card-grid.png.action: The user observes the cluster card grid
US-3.4-02-card-grid.png.expected: Cards for each research theme are displayed in a grid layout

US-3.4-03-card-detail.png.action: The user observes a single theme cluster card
US-3.4-03-card-detail.png.expected: The card shows the cluster name, researcher count, sub-theme tags, and a colored border

US-3.4-04-expanded.png.action: The user clicks on a theme cluster card to expand it
US-3.4-04-expanded.png.expected: The card expands to reveal member researcher names as clickable links

US-3.4-05-navigate-profile.png.action: The user clicks a researcher name inside an expanded theme card
US-3.4-05-navigate-profile.png.expected: The application navigates to that researcher's profile page

US-3.4-06-crossnav-researchers.png.action: The user clicks the See All Researchers link in an expanded card
US-3.4-06-crossnav-researchers.png.expected: The application navigates to the researchers list filtered by that theme

US-3.4-07-crossnav-map.png.action: The user clicks the View on Map button at the top of the themes page
US-3.4-07-crossnav-map.png.expected: The application navigates to the thematic map page

US-3.4-E1-01-intercept.png.action: The system intercepts the clusters API to return an empty list
US-3.4-E1-01-intercept.png.expected: The API is configured to respond with no cluster data

US-3.4-E1-02-loaded.png.action: The user navigates to the themes page with no clusters available

US-3.4-E1-03-empty-state.png.action: The user observes the themes page when no themes exist
US-3.4-E1-03-empty-state.png.expected: An empty state message is displayed centered on the page

US-3.4-E2-01-intercept.png.action: The system intercepts cluster data to include a cluster with zero members
US-3.4-E2-01-intercept.png.expected: The API is configured to return a cluster with an empty researcher list

US-3.4-E2-02-loaded.png.action: The user navigates to the themes page with an empty cluster present
US-3.4-E2-02-loaded.png.expected: The themes page loads with all cluster cards visible

US-3.4-E2-03-zero-members.png.action: The user observes the card for a cluster with no researchers
US-3.4-E2-03-zero-members.png.expected: The card shows zero researchers and cannot be expanded

US-3.4-E3-01-intercept.png.action: The system intercepts the clusters API to return malformed data
US-3.4-E3-01-intercept.png.expected: The API is configured to respond with invalid JSON

US-3.4-E3-02-loaded.png.action: The user navigates to the themes page while the API returns bad data

US-3.4-E3-03-error.png.action: The user observes the themes page after a data parsing failure
US-3.4-E3-03-error.png.expected: An error message with a retry button is shown in place of the card grid

US-1.4-01-stats-loaded.png.action: The user opens the detailed statistics page
US-1.4-01-stats-loaded.png.expected: The stats page loads with a heading and chart sections visible

US-1.4-02-breadcrumb.png.action: The user observes the breadcrumb navigation at the top of the stats page
US-1.4-02-breadcrumb.png.expected: A breadcrumb trail showing the path back to the dashboard is visible

US-1.4-03-bar-chart.png.action: The user observes the bar chart on the stats page
US-1.4-03-bar-chart.png.expected: A bar chart displays theme distribution with theme names and counts on the axes

US-1.4-04-bar-tooltip.png.action: The user hovers over a bar in the bar chart
US-1.4-04-bar-tooltip.png.expected: A tooltip appears showing the exact researcher count for that theme

US-1.4-05-line-chart.png.action: The user observes the line chart on the stats page
US-1.4-05-line-chart.png.expected: A line chart shows temporal trends with time on the x-axis and count on the y-axis

US-1.4-06-histogram.png.action: The user observes the histogram on the stats page
US-1.4-06-histogram.png.expected: A histogram shows similarity score distribution across score ranges

US-1.4-07-back-dashboard.png.action: The user clicks the Dashboard link in the breadcrumb
US-1.4-07-back-dashboard.png.expected: The application navigates back to the main dashboard

US-1.4-E1-01-intercept.png.action: The system intercepts the detailed stats API to return empty data arrays
US-1.4-E1-01-intercept.png.expected: The API is configured to respond with no chart data

US-1.4-E1-02-loaded.png.action: The user navigates to the stats page with no data available
US-1.4-E1-02-loaded.png.expected: The stats page loads with its layout

US-1.4-E1-03-empty-charts.png.action: The user observes the charts when no data is available
US-1.4-E1-03-empty-charts.png.expected: All three charts show a message indicating insufficient data to generate the graph

US-1.4-E2-01-intercept.png.action: The system intercepts the stats API to return data for only one researcher
US-1.4-E2-01-intercept.png.expected: The API is configured with a single researcher and no similarity data

US-1.4-E2-02-loaded.png.action: The user navigates to the stats page with only one researcher in the system
US-1.4-E2-02-loaded.png.expected: The stats page loads with the bar and line charts visible

US-1.4-E2-03-histogram-message.png.action: The user observes the histogram when only one researcher exists
US-1.4-E2-03-histogram-message.png.expected: The histogram shows a message that at least two researchers are required for similarity

US-1.4-E3-01-intercept.png.action: The system intercepts the detailed stats API to return malformed data
US-1.4-E3-01-intercept.png.expected: The API is configured to respond with invalid JSON

US-1.4-E3-02-loaded.png.action: The user navigates to the stats page while the API returns bad data

US-1.4-E3-03-error-state.png.action: The user observes the charts after a data parsing failure
US-1.4-E3-03-error-state.png.expected: Each chart section shows an error message with a retry link
